rationale: Wraps `localStorage` access in both `src/lib/access.ts` and the generated `src/lib/access.js` with a `getStorage()` helper that returns `null` when `window` is undefined or when accessing `window.localStorage` throws (Safari private mode, iframe sandboxing, etc.). Every call site (`hasTrafficApproval`, `saveAccessRequest`, `getAccessRequest`, `approveTrafficWithCode`, `clearTrafficApproval`) is updated to use optional chaining `getStorage()?.getItem/setItem/removeItem`, so the approval gate degrades gracefully instead of crashing. The PR body's broader claims about bot management UI, OTP, and contact sync are not present in this slice of the diff, but the storage-hardening + add/add conflict resolution it lists for `access.ts`/`access.js` is implemented correctly. The TypeScript and emitted JS stay in sync (same function-by-function transformation), no test changes, no hallucinated APIs.
